status: exact classical-field balance reduction with fixed-time compactness gate
proof_boundary: This artifact proves exact principal-value, perturbation, arithmetic-lattice, and continuum-density identities; imports the published fixed-positive-time high-zero localization theorem with its nonuniform threshold; and blocks promotion from macroscopic zero locations to collision-field control. It does not prove the needed lambda-uniform reciprocal-gap balance, exclude a compact positive-boundary collision, prove RH, or prove Lambda <= 0.

exact.principal_value_field: For H(z)=(z-c)^2*V(z), B_c=V'(c)/V(c)=PV sum_(signed roots y outside the pair) 1/(c-y).
exact.arithmetic_pair_equilibrium: For roots c+(j+1/2)h, j in Z, removing the pair c+/-h/2 gives PV sum 1/(c-y)=0 by termwise reflection.
exact.weighted_stability: |B(c;y)-B(c0;r)| <= sum_j |delta_j-delta_c|/(|c-y_j|*|c0-r_j|), provided the paired sums converge.

published_fixed_time_localization.theorem: For each fixed 0<t<=1/2, sufficiently high zeros are real, simple, and uniquely localized near the quantiles g(x_n,t)=n once x_n>=exp(C/t).
published_fixed_time_localization.consequence: At fixed positive t, every multiple zero lies in a bounded region |x|<exp(C/t), after adjusting the absolute constant.
published_fixed_time_localization.uniformity_warning: The localization scale diverges exponentially as t decreases to zero.

macroscopic_location_countermodels.reference: Signed integer lattice with double roots at +/-n; after removing the +n pair its field is 1/(2*n).
macroscopic_location_countermodels.guard: Even symmetry and uniformly bounded location error do not bound the collision field or stiffness.

exact.compactness_reduction: If Lambda>0, the published fixed-time theorem confines every boundary multiple zero at t=Lambda to |x|<exp(C/Lambda); closing Lambda<=0 still requires a lambda-uniform analytic exclusion of that expanding compact region.
exact.open_handoff: Prove a lambda-uniform paired reciprocal-gap discrepancy bound strong enough for the weighted perturbation sum, together with an explicit far-tail bound, or prove a different analytic exclusion that remains effective as lambda->0+.

rows.claim: The regularized field at a double zero is the principal-value signed-root field with the colliding pair removed.
rows.formula: For H(z)=(z-c)^2*V(z), B_c=V'(c)/V(c)=PV sum_(signed roots y outside the pair) 1/(c-y).
rows.proof_boundary: Canonical-product identity at a real-zero time.

rows.claim: An arithmetic lattice has exactly zero external field at the midpoint of a removed adjacent pair.
rows.formula: For roots c+(j+1/2)h, j in Z, removing the pair c+/-h/2 gives PV sum 1/(c-y)=0 by termwise reflection.

rows.claim: Field displacement from any reference configuration is governed exactly by a weighted relative-location sum.
rows.formula: If c=c0+delta_c and y_j=r_j+delta_j, then 1/(c-y_j)-1/(c0-r_j)=(delta_j-delta_c)/((c-y_j)(c0-r_j)). Hence |B(c;y)-B(c0;r)| <= sum_j |delta_j-delta_c|/(|c-y_j|*|c0-r_j|), provided the paired sums converge.
rows.proof_boundary: Requires convergent paired sums and nonzero outsider gaps.

rows.claim: The continuum field generated by the Riemann-von Mangoldt density tends to -pi/8.
rows.proof_boundary: Continuum density benchmark, not the discrete Xi zero field.

rows.claim: Implicit differentiation of the positive-time quantile law gives velocity -pi/4 and field -pi/8 at high height.
The published positive-time asymptotic theorem makes every sufficiently high zero simple and uniquely localized at fixed t>0.
rows.formula: For each fixed 0<t<=1/2, sufficiently high zeros are real, simple, and uniquely localized near the quantiles g(x_n,t)=n once x_n>=exp(C/t).
rows.proof_boundary: External theorem for each fixed positive time; its threshold is not uniform as t approaches zero.

diagnostics.theorem: For each fixed 0<t<=1/2, sufficiently high zeros are real, simple, and uniquely localized near the quantiles g(x_n,t)=n once x_n>=exp(C/t).
diagnostics.consequence: At fixed positive t, every multiple zero lies in a bounded region |x|<exp(C/t), after adjusting the absolute constant.
diagnostics.uniformity_warning: The localization scale diverges exponentially as t decreases to zero.

rows.claim: A hypothetical positive Newman boundary can have multiple zeros only in a t-dependent compact height range.
rows.formula: If Lambda>0, the published fixed-time theorem confines every boundary multiple zero at t=Lambda to |x|<exp(C/Lambda); closing Lambda<=0 still requires a lambda-uniform analytic exclusion of that expanding compact region.
rows.proof_boundary: Conditional on Lambda>0; does not exclude the compact collision.

rows.claim: Even symmetry and bounded error from classical locations do not control the sign or magnitude of the collision field.
rows.formula: One neighboring even pair moved by <1 makes B tend to either sign of infinity.
rows.proof_boundary: Abstract lattice countermodels, not Xi zero configurations.

diagnostics.reference: Signed integer lattice with double roots at +/-n; after removing the +n pair its field is 1/(2*n).
diagnostics.guard: Even symmetry and uniformly bounded location error do not bound the collision field or stiffness.

rows.claim: Riemann-von Mangoldt counting and macroscopic location estimates must be upgraded to reciprocal-gap weighted local discrepancy control.
rows.formula: |B(c;y)-B(c0;r)| <= sum_j |delta_j-delta_c|/(|c-y_j|*|c0-r_j|), provided the paired sums converge.
rows.proof_boundary: Necessity for this perturbative field route, not for every possible RH proof.

rows.claim: The fixed-time compactness theorem closes the high tail only after a lambda-uniform local balance or compact-region exclusion is supplied.
rows.formula: Prove a lambda-uniform paired reciprocal-gap discrepancy bound strong enough for the weighted perturbation sum, together with an explicit far-tail bound, or prove a different analytic exclusion that remains effective as lambda->0+.
rows.proof_boundary: Open lambda-uniform theorem; not a proof of RH or Lambda <= 0.
